---
title: "Listening"
---

# Listening Across Eras

Because this project focuses on video game music, this page provides a brief listening companion to the timeline and data analysis. These examples illustrate how soundtrack style changed across technological eras.

## Early / 8-Bit Era

Early game music relied on short looping melodies and a limited number of sound channels.

### Super Mario Bros. (1985)

<iframe width="100%%" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/iy3qq7zc4EY?si=wzBRXC5J1e3rBTpG" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

This example highlights the short-loop structure and simple electronic timbres associated with early hardware limitations.

---

## 16-Bit / Expanded Console Era

Improved sound chips and sampling allowed for richer textures and more stylistic variety.

### Sonic the Hedgehog (1991)

<iframe width="100%" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/G-i8HYi1QH0?si=KgK-hGO3Dv_wlEqU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

This track reflects the faster rhythms and expanded musical palette of the 16-bit era.

---

## CD / Early 3D Era

As storage expanded, soundtracks became clearer, longer, and more cinematic.

### Ocarina of Time (1998)

<iframe width="100%" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/1y5GnZb9J1k?si=hCQM_HxB8XlYydi5" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

This example shows how music increasingly supported atmosphere, world-building, and emotional tone.

---

## Modern Era

Modern games include both large orchestral scores and more ambient, minimalist approaches.

### Halo: Combat Evolved (2001)

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/OpkSer8CNsA?si=umSzzBkQZyHMSpXN" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Halo demonstrates the rise of cinematic orchestral scoring in modern games.

### Revert to minimalism

<iframe width="100%" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/5w3rRFWzjcM?si=BzILKHi4bZj9MbYr" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Minecraft offers a different modern approach: minimalist ambient composition used to create reflection and atmosphere.
